title: De-duplicate the event stream
prompt: The event stream logs some events more than once: the same (user_id, event_name, event_at) shows up in multiple rows — each with its own event_id, so the rows are not byte-for-byte identical. Return the number of unique events, where uniqueness is defined by (user_id, event_name, event_at). Single column unique_events.

generic: SELECT DISTINCT user_id, event_name, event_at FROM events

hints: COUNT(*) counts duplicate rows too — you need distinct combinations.
Count rows from SELECT DISTINCT user_id, event_name, event_at.
